steps.title: Extract the linear model from the graph
steps.content: Two readable points from the graph: $(0,\, 65)$ and $(100,\, 105)$. The slope is $$\frac{105 - 65}{100 - 0} = \frac{40}{100} = \frac{2}{5}.$$ So the model is $t(x) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}x$.

steps.title: Check the candidate (slope = 2/5)
steps.content:
For every increase of $x$ by 1, $t(x)$ increases by $\frac{2}{5}$. The slope is exactly $\frac{2}{5}$.
  • "For each increase of $x$ by $1$, $t(x)$ increases by approximately $\frac{2}{5}$": TRUE — eliminate.

steps.title: Check the candidate (GC = 0% gives 60-65°C)
steps.content:
At $x = 0$: $t(0) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(0) = 65^\circ C$. The value $65^\circ C$ falls in the interval $(60^\circ C,\, 65^\circ C]$.
  • "A DNA molecule with a GC content of $0\%$ has an estimated melting temperature between $60^\circ C$ and $65^\circ C$.": TRUE — eliminate.

steps.title: Check the candidate (GC = 95% gives 100-105°C) and the remaining alternative (GC = 98% gives 80-90°C)
steps.content:
At $x = 95$: $t(95) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(95) = 65 + 38 = 103^\circ C$. That is between $100^\circ C$ and $105^\circ C$, so that statement is true.

At $x = 98$: $t(98) = 65 + \frac{2}{5}(98) = 65 + 39.2 = 104.2^\circ C$. The remaining alternative claims this temperature is between $80^\circ C$ and $90^\circ C$, but $104.2^\circ C$ is far outside that range.
  • "A DNA molecule with a GC content of $95\%$ has an estimated melting temperature between $100^\circ C$ and $105^\circ C$.": TRUE — eliminate.
  • "A DNA molecule with a GC content of $98\%$ has an estimated melting temperature between $80^\circ C$ and $90^\circ C$.": FALSE. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
